nodes.context: Italian-American organized-crime group that became a faction of the Gambino crime family, operating in Baltimore, Maryland from about 1900 until the 1990s.
New York-based Mafia family that took over the Baltimore Crew in 1955.
Original leaders of the Baltimore Crew until the Corbi takeover in the 1920s.
Family that took over the Baltimore Crew in the 1920s and functionally headed it after 1920.
Italian crime organization; a reputed Camorra member was murdered by Patsy Corbi in 1923.

edges.evidence_quote: The Baltimore Crew was an Italian-American organized-crime group that ultimately became a faction of the Gambino crime family
edges.context: The Baltimore Crew became a faction of the Gambino crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: It was originally an independent organization, led by the D'Urso family until the Corbi takeover in the 1920s.
edges.context: The D'Urso family originally led the Baltimore Crew.

edges.evidence_quote: The Corbi family acquiesced to the Gambino relationship, but maintained local leaders, simply answering to and accessing support from Morici and his New York Gambino connections.
edges.context: The Corbi family functionally headed the Baltimore Crew after 1920.

edges.evidence_quote: Patsy Corbi was sentenced to life imprisonment for the murder of local barber Frank Naples, a reputed Camorra member.
edges.context: A Corbi family member murdered a reputed Camorra member.
